stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Telegraph operators relayed crucial messages over long-distance wires in the nineteenth century.
  • Before widespread electrification, telegraph offices were staffed by skilled operators who keyed and decoded Morse code.
  • Sarah Bagley was the chief operator at the Lowell telegraph office in Massachusetts.
  • She held this position from 1846 to 1855.
  • Mary Macaulay was the chief operator at the Halifax telegraph office in Nova Scotia.
  • She held this position from 1869 to 1898.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the order in which the two telegraph operators began their careers. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Mary Macaulay began her career as a chief telegraph operator years after Sarah Bagley did.
From 1846 to 1898, long-distance communication in North America was kept reliable thanks to telegraph operators Mary Macaulay and Sarah Bagley.
Before widespread electrification, telegraph operators like Sarah Bagley and Mary Macaulay were crucial to long-distance communication.
Sarah Bagley's career as a chief telegraph operator ended in 1855, whereas Mary Macaulay's ended in 1898.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes the order in which the two telegraph operators began their careers, noting that Macaulay, who became chief operator at Halifax in 1869, began her career years after Bagley, who became chief operator at Lowell in 1846.

Choice B is incorrect. The sentence makes a generalization about the effects of Bagley's and Macaulay's work; it does not emphasize the order in which they began their careers. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence claims that the operators were crucial to long-distance communication; it does not emphasize the order in which they began their careers. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence emphasizes when their respective careers ended; it does not emphasize the order in which they began their careers.
